pytorch.reason: Tensor substrate for node features, edge indices, and GCN weight matrices; torch.linalg.norm used to compute Frobenius deltas between output tensors

pyg.reason: GCNConv MessagePassing computes neighbourhood-aggregated node embeddings on the Hopf-fiber graph; the admitted node set is defined by thresholding GCN output norms, making PyG load-bearing for the admissibility claim

z3.reason: numeric graph structure probe does not require SMT proof layer

cvc5.reason: not needed; admissibility criterion is empirical, not symbolic

sympy.reason: Hopf fiber distance formula is closed-form; no symbolic algebra needed

clifford.reason: quaternion operations implemented directly in torch; clifford not needed

geomstats.reason: S3 sampling done analytically; geomstats not required here

e3nn.reason: SO(3) equivariance not the focus of this Hopf-graph structure probe

rustworkx.reason: graph representation handled entirely by PyG edge_index tensors

xgi.reason: hypergraph structure not needed; pairwise edges only

toponetx.reason: cell-complex topology not needed; this probe targets graph-level structure

gudhi.reason: persistent homology not required for this graph-structure test

pos_a_structure_changes_admissibility.note: GCN message passing over Hopf-fiber edges must change the admitted node set

pos_c_sparse_vs_full_distinct.note: Hopf-sparse and fully-connected graphs must yield different admitted node sets

neg_a_identity_gcn_no_delta.note: Zero-weight GCN should not show >10% delta — confirms delta is due to message passing

neg_b_random_graph_differs.note: Random graphs at same density must not reproduce exact Hopf admissibility partition

boundary_a_near_zero_threshold.note: Near-zero threshold yields disconnected graph; GCN degenerates to no-edge baseline

boundary_b_near_full_threshold.note: Large threshold approaches fully-connected; GCN output should converge to full-graph output
